sections.title: Portugal Real Estate
sections.contentHtml:
Compliance Policy 2026

Expert Edition for Real Estate Marketplace Platforms

Verified & Updated — March 16, 2026

DOCUMENT PURPOSE: This document is the authoritative compliance reference for real estate marketplace platforms operating in Portugal as of March 2026. It covers agent licensing, portal rules, taxes, Alojamento Local regulations, privacy law, and transaction processes.

CRITICAL STRUCTURAL DIFFERENCE: Portugal has NO Multiple Listing Service (MLS). There is no IDX, VOW, or centralized listing database equivalent to the U.S. system. Listings are distributed by agencies and private sellers independently across competing commercial portals.

sections.title: Market Structure and Fundamental Differences
sections.contentHtml:
The Portuguese real estate market operates on a fundamentally different architecture from the U.S. MLS-based system. There is no centralized listing database, no mandatory data sharing protocol between competing agencies, and no national IDX or VOW framework. Listings are distributed by individual agencies and private sellers across a fragmented ecosystem of competing commercial portals, with each agency choosing which portals to list on and under what terms.

TopicUSA (MLS-based)
Portugal (Portal-based)Centralized database
YES — regional MLS per areaNO — fragmented across portals
Data sharing obligationYES — IDX/VOW rules mandatory
NO — agencies choose portals freelySold price transparency
YES in most statesPARTIAL — AT registry has records; portals show asking price only
Licensing bodyNAR (national) + state boards
IMPIC (national regulator)Listing exclusivity rule
CCP mandates broad exposureAgency contract type governs (exclusive vs. non-exclusive)
CompensationPost-settlement 2024 reforms
Typically 5–6% paid by seller; buyer agents less commonFeed standards
RESO Web API, OData, JSONNo national feed standard — each portal has own API

sections.title: Regulatory Bodies
sections.contentHtml:
Portugal's real estate market is supervised by a set of distinct regulatory bodies, each with a specific mandate. Marketplace platforms must maintain awareness of and compliance with each body's requirements.

IMPIC — Primary Real Estate Regulator

The Instituto dos Mercados Públicos, do Imobiliário e da Construção (IMPIC) is the primary government regulator for real estate agents, agencies, and construction in Portugal. IMPIC is responsible for issuing, managing, and enforcing the AMI license (Licença AMI) — the mandatory operating license for all real estate professionals. IMPIC also supervises public procurement and the construction sector, falling under the Ministry of Infrastructure and Housing.

* Official website: impic.pt

* AMI license verification: impic.pt → 'Verificar número AMI' → enter license number

* Complaints against agents filed directly with IMPIC

CNPD — Data Protection Authority

The Comissão Nacional de Proteção de Dados (CNPD) is Portugal's independent data protection authority. It supervises and enforces the EU's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the Portuguese Data Protection Law (Lei n.º 58/2019). Real estate marketplace platforms processing user data are classified as data controllers and are directly subject to CNPD oversight.

* Official website: cnpd.pt

* Governing law: GDPR (EU 2016/679) + Lei n.º 58/2019

* Serious violations: fines up to EUR 20 million or 4% of global annual turnover, whichever is higher

* Largest fine to date in Portugal: EUR 4.3 million (Statistics institute INE, December 2022)

* 2025–2026 enforcement focus: unsolicited electronic marketing communications — high-risk sector

AT — Tax Authority

The Autoridade Tributária e Aduaneira (AT) administers all property-related taxes, including the Municipal Property Transfer Tax (IMT), Annual Municipal Property Tax (IMI), Additional Property Wealth Tax (AIMI), and Stamp Duty. All IMT and Stamp Duty payments are made via the Portal das Finanças before deed signing. AT also holds the official VPT (Valor Patrimonial Tributário) for all properties, which serves as the taxable base for multiple calculations.

* Portal: portaldasfinancas.gov.pt

AIMA — Immigration Authority (New 2024)

The Agência para a Integração, Migrações e Asilo (AIMA) replaced the former SEF (Serviço de Estrangeiros e Fronteiras), which was dissolved. AIMA administers foreign national registration for Alojamento Local (AL) operators, who are required to report all non-Portuguese guest stays via the SIBA platform under AIMA's authority.

Turismo de Portugal

Turismo de Portugal processes AL (short-term rental) registrations via the national Balcão Único Eletrónico (BUE) portal at ePortugal.gov.pt. AL operator insurance details are publicly accessible on the Turismo de Portugal website, allowing guests and municipalities to verify coverage.

________________

sections.title: Agent Licensing — The AMI License
sections.contentHtml:
All real estate agents and agencies operating in Portugal must hold a valid AMI license (Licença AMI) issued by IMPIC. Operating without a valid AMI license is illegal and exposes the individual or company to civil and criminal penalties. For marketplace platforms, the AMI license is the single most important credential to verify when onboarding agency partners.

AMI License Requirements

RequirementDetail
Governing lawLei n.º 15/2013, of February 8
Issued byIMPIC
Mandatory forAll real estate agents and agencies mediating property transactions in Portugal
Civil liability insuranceMinimum EUR 150,000 — mandatory for all licensed agencies
Initial registration feeEUR 50
Annual activity feeEUR 265
Second copy of license cardEUR 25
Display requirementAMI number must appear on website, promotional materials, business cards, and ALL marketing materials
Change notificationAgency must notify IMPIC of ALL changes to registration data (Article 20, Lei 15/2013)
Contract registerAgency must maintain an updated register of all mediation contracts signed (Article 20)
Complaints bookLivro de reclamações mandatory in all physical establishments

Verifying an AMI License

Platforms must verify each agency's AMI status before granting listing access. The process is straightforward: visit impic.pt, navigate to 'Verificar número AMI', enter the license number, and confirm active status and valid insurance. An AMI number that returns no result or shows 'suspended' status must not be onboarded.

APEMIP — Professional Association

The Associação dos Profissionais e Empresas de Mediação Imobiliária de Portugal (APEMIP) is a voluntary professional association for real estate practitioners. APEMIP membership is not required for legal operation — IMPIC registration is the mandatory credential. However, APEMIP membership signals professional commitment and adherence to a code of ethics.

sections.title: Listing Portal Landscape
sections.contentHtml:
In the absence of an MLS, Portugal's property market is distributed across a competitive ecosystem of listing portals. Unlike the U.S. system where IDX feeds create a standardized data layer, each Portuguese portal operates independently with its own proprietary feed formats, API specifications, and content policies.

Major Portals — Reference Table

PortalURL
PositionKey Notes
Idealista Portugalidealista.pt
#1 — LargestSpain-origin platform. Available in 15+ languages. Free for private sellers. Requires AMI display for all agency listings.
Imovirtualimovirtual.com
#2Local favourite, strong in rentals. Paid listings auto-cross-post to OLX (same OLX Group ownership).
OLX Imóveisolx.pt
#2–3 (general classifieds)General classifieds platform. Private and agency listings. Less professional oversight than specialist portals.
Casa Sapocasa.sapo.pt
#4Legacy portal established 2001. Strong agency partnerships. Also branded as SuperCasa (mobile app).
Green Acresgreen-acres.pt
#5Primarily targets international buyers. English-friendly interface. Rural, villa, and luxury focus.
BPI Expresso Imobiliáriobpiexpressoimobiliario.pt
#6Bank-owned. Specialist in bank-repossessed properties. High-value listings.
REMAX Portugalremax.pt
Agency #1 brand siteREMAX Portugal brand portal. Separate from general listing portals above.
ERA Portugalera.pt
Agency #2 brand siteERA Portugal brand portal. Strong nationwide coverage.
Pure Portugalpureportugal.co.uk
NicheSpecialist in rural land, farms, and renovation projects. Not a mainstream general portal.
Custo Justocustojusto.pt
ClassifiedsBudget classifieds. Peer-to-peer. Low agency presence. No AMI verification.

Portal Compliance Rules

* AMI number must be displayed on all agency listings across all portals — this is a national legal requirement, not a portal policy.

* There is no national feed standard equivalent to RESO. Each portal has its own API and feed format. Platforms must build per-portal integrations.

* The same property is frequently listed by multiple agencies simultaneously on the same portal, which is legal under non-exclusive agency contracts.

* Sold prices are not displayed on any portal. Only asking prices are shown. Actual sale prices are held by the AT (tax authority).

* Imovirtual paid listings automatically cross-post to OLX due to shared OLX Group ownership.

* Private individuals may list on Idealista (free), OLX, and Custo Justo without an AMI license.

sections.title: Agency Contracts and Mediation Agreements
sections.contentHtml:
Types of Mediation Contract

Contract TypePortuguese Name
ExclusivityKey Rules
ExclusiveContrato de mediação imobiliária exclusivo
YES — seller cannot engage other agencies during the termAgency holds sole rights for agreed period. Most common for premium and luxury listings.
Non-exclusiveContrato de mediação imobiliária não exclusivo
NO — seller may engage multiple agencies simultaneouslyLegal cause of portal duplication. Multiple agencies list the same property on the same portal.
Open listing (informal)Listagem aberta
NO — informal arrangementNo formal contract. Agency paid only on successful sale. Uncommon for professional agencies.

CPCV — Promissory Contract

The Contrato Promessa de Compra e Venda (CPCV) is a legally binding promissory contract signed between buyer and seller before the final deed. While not required by law, the CPCV is used in the overwhelming majority of Portuguese property transactions and is strongly recommended. It locks in the agreed price, conditions, and timeline, and carries significant financial penalties for withdrawal.

* Deposit (sinal): typically 10–30% of the purchase price, paid by the buyer at CPCV signing.

* Buyer withdraws without legal grounds: deposit is forfeited to the seller.

* Seller withdraws without legal grounds: seller must pay the buyer double the deposit amount.

* Funds are held in the buyer's lawyer's client account — never in the agent's account.

* IMT (transfer tax) must be paid within approximately 30 days of CPCV signing, before the final deed.

* A Power of Attorney (procuração) may be granted to allow a lawyer to sign on behalf of a remote buyer.

Escritura — Final Deed

The Escritura Pública de Compra e Venda is the formal deed of sale, executed before a notary at a Cartório Notarial. The notary confirms all legal preconditions are met, presides over the signing, and issues the deed. Following execution, the transaction must be registered with the Portuguese Land Registry (Conservatória do Registo Predial) and notified to the AT.

* Proof of IMT payment required before signing — notary will not proceed without it.

* Proof of Stamp Duty payment required at signing.

* Remaining balance of purchase price is paid by certified bank cheque (cheque bancário).

sections.title: Standard Transaction Process
sections.contentHtml:
The standard Portuguese property purchase follows a defined sequence. Platforms building workflow tools, compliance checkers, or transaction management features must reflect this structure accurately.

StepAction
PartyKey Compliance Point
1Obtain NIF (fiscal number)
BuyerRequired before any contract, bank account, or property ownership. Foreign buyers obtain at Finanças office or via lawyer with power of attorney.
2Property search via portals
BuyerVerify agent's AMI number on IMPIC before engaging. Check insurance status.
3Legal due diligence
Buyer's lawyerPull Certidão Permanente (land registry certificate). Check for mortgages, debts, easements. Verify caderneta predial.
4Sign CPCV + pay deposit
Buyer + SellerDeposit (10–30%) held in lawyer's escrow. Double-deposit penalty applies for seller default.
5Pay IMT via Portal das Finanças
BuyerMust be paid before deed signing. Notary will not proceed without proof of payment.
6Pay Stamp Duty
BuyerFlat 0.8% of purchase price (or VPT if higher). Paid to notary at deed signing.
7Sign Escritura at notary
Buyer + Seller + NotaryBalance paid by certified bank cheque. Notary issues deed.
8Register at Land Registry + AT
Buyer's lawyerConservatória do Registo Predial. Also notify AT of new ownership.
9IMI begins next calendar year
Buyer (new owner)IMI billed to owner as of 31 December of purchase year. First bill arrives in the following year.

Required Documents for Deed Signing

The notary will require all of the following documents to be in order before proceeding with the Escritura. Platforms building transaction management tools should use this as a document checklist:

* Civil and tax identification documents of all parties

* Certidão Permanente (land registry extract — proves ownership and any encumbrances)

* Caderneta Predial (property tax booklet from AT — shows VPT and official property data)

* Licença de Utilização (user/habitation licence — required for buildings constructed after August 1951)

* Ficha Técnica de Habitação (building data sheet — required if user licence issued after 30 March 2004)

* Certificado Energético (energy performance certificate — mandatory for all transactions)

* Certidão de Infraestruturas (if Plot Partitioning Licence registered since 1992)

* Proof of IMT payment

* Proof of Stamp Duty payment

* CPCV (if signed)

sections.title: Tax Reference — Property Transactions
sections.contentHtml:
Portugal imposes several taxes on property acquisition, ownership, and disposal. Understanding each tax's rate, calculation basis, and timing is essential for compliance platforms, listing tools, and any AVM or cost-calculator features.

IMT — Municipal Property Transfer Tax

The Imposto Municipal sobre Transmissões Onerosas de Imóveis (IMT) is the transaction tax paid by the buyer on all property purchases. It must be paid via the Portal das Finanças before the deed is signed. The notary will not proceed without proof of payment.

CategoryRate / Structure
Key NotesCalculation basis
Higher of purchase price or VPT (official tax value)Buyer cannot negotiate around VPT for tax purposes.
Primary residence (resident)Progressive scale up to 7% with deductibles
Marginal rates apply; effective rate is typically lower than the headline.Secondary / holiday home
Progressive scale — higher effective rateLess favourable bracket structure than primary residence.
Rural landFlat 5%
Applies to rustic/agricultural properties.Non-resident buyer (2026)
7.5% flat rate on purchase priceSimplified 2026 reform. Replaces prior progressive scale for non-residents.
First buyer under 35 — full exemption0% IMT AND 0% Stamp Duty if: first property + primary residence + buyer under 35 + price does not exceed EUR 330,539 (2026 threshold, updated from EUR 324,058)
Full IMT and Stamp Duty exemption. Most impactful relief for young buyers. Stamp Duty exemption added August 2024 — applies alongside IMT relief.Couple — one partner over 35
50% IMT and 50% Stamp Duty reductionApplies where only one partner qualifies for the under-35 relief. Both reliefs apply proportionally.
Urban rehabilitationPossible exemption
Properties bought for renovation in eligible urban rehabilitation areas.Professional resale
Possible exemptionFor licensed property dealers reselling within defined period.

b Full 2026 IMT Rate Table — Urban Residential Properties

The 2026 State Budget adjusted all IMT brackets upward by 2% in line with the expected inflation rate. The following is the full rate table for urban properties intended exclusively for housing:

Property Value (2026)IMT Rate (Owner-Occupied Primary Residence)
IMT Rate (Secondary / Other Residential)Up to €106,346
0% — exempt1%
€106,347 to €145,2772%
2%€145,278 to €197,698
5%5%
€197,699 to €272,9837%
7%€272,984 to €550,836
8%8%
€550,837 to €1,102,9206% (flat — no progression above this)
6%Above €1,102,920
7.5% flat rate on full value7.5% flat
Non-resident buyer (2026)7.5% flat on full purchase price (new for 2026 — excludes Portuguese emigrants)
7.5% flatUnder-35 first buyer — up to €330,539
0% (full IMT exemption)N/A — only primary residence qualifies
Under-35 first buyer — €330,540 to €660,982Partial: 8% on excess above €330,539
N/AUnder-35 first buyer — above €660,982
Standard rates — no exemptionN/A

c Public Guarantee for Young Buyers (Garantia Pública) — 2026

In August 2024, the Portuguese government introduced a state-backed mortgage guarantee programme for young first-time buyers. In January 2026, the government reinforced the programme with an additional EUR 275.8 million in guarantees (EUR 250M to CGD and EUR 25.8M to Banco CTT), bringing the total programme size to EUR 1.55 billion. This programme is directly relevant to marketplace platforms serving the Portuguese residential market, as it materially affects buyer affordability and the addressable market for properties priced between EUR 200,000 and EUR 450,000.

FeatureDetail
Programme nameGarantia Pública para Habitação / Public Guarantee for Housing
Eligible buyersAges 18 to 35 (inclusive). Only one partner in a couple needs to be 35 or under.
Property typeFirst permanent primary residence only. Not for construction, renovation, or investment.
Maximum property valueEUR 450,000 — transactions above this value do not qualify
State guarantee amountUp to 15% of the purchase price (not the full loan)
Effect on LTVWith the 15% state guarantee, banks can offer 100% LTV — buyers need no cash deposit
Income capAnnual taxable income must not exceed the 8th IRS bracket (approx. EUR 80,000/year)
AML / tax statusNo outstanding debts to the Tax Authority (AT) or Social Security
One-time benefitEach taxpayer may benefit from this guarantee only once
Bank participationOptional for banks — not all banks participate. Participating banks include CGD, Novo Banco, BPI, Bankinter, Banco Montepio, Banco CTT and others.
Guarantee validity10 years from contract signing date. Proportionally reduced on partial early repayments.
Contract deadlineMust be signed by 31 December 2026 (extendable by government decision)
Foreign residentsEligible if tax resident in Portugal regardless of nationality
Can be combined withIMT and Stamp Duty exemptions for under-35 first buyers — these are cumulative benefits
Does NOT coverInterest, bank fees, construction or renovation loans, investment purchases
Government still approves buyer creditworthinessYES — bank always conducts standard credit risk assessment. State guarantee does not override credit scoring.
PLATFORM SIGNIFICANCE: The combination of IMT exemption (up to €330,539) + Stamp Duty exemption + 0% deposit via Public Guarantee means a qualifying buyer under 35 can purchase a property up to €330,539 with no upfront costs except notary and legal fees. Platforms should prominently surface this eligibility information on qualifying listings. 

Stamp Duty — Imposto do Selo

SituationRate
Property purchase0.8% — flat rate on higher of purchase price or VPT
Mortgage (term over 5 years)0.6% on mortgage loan amount
Mortgage (term under 5 years)0.5% on mortgage loan amount
Inheritance — direct family (spouse, children, parents, grandchildren)0.8%
Inheritance — all other beneficiaries10.8% total (0.8% standard + 10% additional)
Gift between direct family0% gift tax — but 0.8% Stamp Duty still applies

IMI — Annual Municipal Property Tax

The Imposto Municipal sobre Imóveis (IMI) is an annual tax levied on property owners by their municipality. The rate is set by each municipality within a national range.

* Calculation basis: VPT (Valor Patrimonial Tributário — typically 30–50% below market value).

* Urban properties: 0.3% to 0.45% (each municipality sets rate within this band).

* Rural properties: 0.8% flat rate.

* Payment: Under EUR 100 — by 31 May. EUR 100–500 — two installments (May + November). Over EUR 500 — three installments (May + August + November).

* Liability: Assessed to the owner as of 31 December of the preceding year. If you buy in December 2025, the first bill arrives in April 2026.

* Exemption: Owners with residential VPT up to EUR 125,000 exempt if annual income is under EUR 153,300.

AIMI — Additional Property Wealth Tax

The Adicional ao IMI (AIMI) is a wealth surcharge on high-value Portuguese residential property portfolios. It applies to individuals and married couples whose total residential VPT across all Portuguese properties exceeds the threshold.

Portfolio VPT (Individual)AIMI Rate
Up to EUR 600,0000% — no AIMI
EUR 600,001 to EUR 1,000,0000.7% on the value in this band
EUR 1,000,001 to EUR 2,000,0001.0% on the value in this band
Above EUR 2,000,0001.5% on the value above EUR 2M
MARRIED COUPLES: The AIMI threshold doubles to EUR 1,200,000 for married couples or civil partners filing jointly. 

Capital Gains Tax

* Non-residents: 28% flat rate on the full capital gain.

* Residents: 50% of the gain is added to annual income and taxed at the individual's progressive IRS rate.

* Primary home exemption (residents only): Gain fully exempt if all proceeds are reinvested in a new EU/EEA primary residence within a defined window.

* Deductible costs that reduce the gain: IMT and Stamp Duty at purchase, documented renovation costs, notary fees, and real estate commission fees.

Agency Commission

* Standard rate: typically 5% to 6% of sale price plus 23% VAT.

* Paid by: the seller in the overwhelming majority of transactions.

* Buyer's agents: less common in Portugal than in the U.S., but present (e.g. Goldcrest). Fee negotiated separately.

sections.title: Alojamento Local (Short-Term Rental) Compliance
sections.contentHtml:
Alojamento Local (AL) is Portugal's regulatory framework for short-term furnished rentals — properties rented for tourism purposes, typically for periods under 30 days. The regulatory environment underwent major reform in late 2024, significantly relaxing the restrictions introduced by the Mais Habitação program of 2023.

Legal Framework — 2026 Status

Decree-Law n.º 76/2024 came into force on November 1, 2024, with full practical effects from January 1, 2025. It reversed the most restrictive aspects of the Mais Habitação program and restored operator confidence in the AL sector.

Change Introduced by DL 76/2024Detail
5-year renewal requirement abolishedAL licences are now permanent — no expiry date
Licence transferability restoredAL licences survive property sales and transfers (subject to municipal containment zone exceptions)
National freeze liftedNational ban on new AL licences lifted as of early 2025
Municipal discretion preservedMunicipalities retain authority to declare containment zones and restrict new licences in saturated areas

AL Application Process

* Method: Prior notification (comunicação prévia / notificação prévia) submitted via the national ePortugal BUE portal (Balcão do Empreendedor).

* Standard response time: up to 60 working days (dias úteis) — approximately 3 calendar months. Prior document versions incorrectly stated this as 60 calendar days.

* Containment zone response time: up to 90 working days (dias úteis) — approximately 4.5 calendar months.

* Mandatory inspection window: 60 working days (standard) or 90 working days (containment zones) — same correction applies.

* If rejected, one review request is allowed — the applicant bears the associated costs.

* National capacity limit: maximum 9 rooms and 27 guests per AL unit.

AMI NOT REQUIRED: AL operators do not need an AMI license. AL is a property use registration, not a real estate brokerage license.

Ongoing Operator Obligations

ObligationRequirement
TimingInsurance
Mandatory commercial landlord insurance covering AL activity. Proof must be produced within 3 days if municipality requests it. Details publicly accessible on Turismo de Portugal website.Before first guest
Complaints bookRequired in both paper and digital format
OngoingForeign guest registration
All non-Portuguese guests must be registered via SIBA platform (AIMA authority) within 3 days of arrival.Within 3 days per stay
Tourist tax collectionCollect and remit taxa turística where the municipality levies it.
Per eligible stayTourist tax remittance
Remit collected tourist tax to the relevant municipality monthly (varies by municipality).Monthly

Tourist Tax Rates by City

CityRate per Person per Night
CapCollection Responsibility
2026 StatusLisbon
EUR 4.007 nights
Airbnb auto-collects and remits. Other OTAs and direct bookings: host must collect and remit monthly.Active
PortoEUR 3.00
7 nightsAirbnb auto-collects as of December 2024. Other OTAs: host responsibility.
Active from Dec 2024Cascais
EUR 2.007 nights
Host collects and remits. Verify current rate with Cascais municipality.Active
SintraEUR 2.00
7 nightsHost collects and remits.
ActiveFaro (Algarve)
EUR 1.507 nights
Host collects and remits.Active
Funchal (Madeira)EUR 2.00
5 nightsHost collects and remits. Regional rules apply.
ActiveOther municipalities
VARIESVARIES
Check with specific municipality. Many have introduced or are considering tourist tax.Verify locally

sections.title: Municipal AL Containment Zones
sections.contentHtml:
While the national freeze on new AL licences was lifted in early 2025, individual municipalities retain the power to declare 'containment zones' where new registrations are restricted or blocked. The rules vary significantly by city and are subject to change. Platforms must verify current containment zone status before processing any new AL registration in a restricted area.

CRITICAL: Always verify current containment zone status directly with the relevant municipality before processing an AL application. Rules change and what was open in one quarter may be blocked in the next.

Lisbon

Lisbon's AL regulatory history is important context for any platform processing historical or current AL applications. On 5 November 2024, Lisbon suspended all new AL registrations citywide. The suspension was extended in May 2025 while the municipality revised its AL regulation. The updated regulation was then formally adopted in November 2025, replacing the blanket suspension with the structured containment zone thresholds described below. Any AL application submitted between November 2024 and November 2025 should be flagged for manual status review.

* Absolute containment trigger: 10% AL ratio at parish or neighborhood level — no new licences permitted.

* Relative containment range: 5–10% AL ratio — restricted and monitored.

* Current citywide ratio: approximately 7.2% (as of March 2026).

Lisbon Parishes/Neighborhoods — New AL Registrations Currently BLOCKED

* Parishes: Santa Maria Maior, Misericórdia, Santo António, São Vicente, Arroios, Estrela

* Neighborhoods: Ajuda, Alcântara, Belém, Bom Sucesso, Parque das Nações, Picoas, São Bento, São Sebastião da Pedreira, Sapadores

* Additional restricted pockets in Alvalade (Entrecampos, Av. Gago Coutinho), Marvila, and Beato

Porto

* Regulation adopted: 10 December 2024.

* Absolute containment threshold: 15% AL ratio per parish.

* New licences blocked in historic center parishes (Bonfim, Cedofeita, Miragaia, etc.).

* Temporary licences may be possible in non-saturated areas — verify per-parish with Porto municipality.

Other Key Municipalities

Municipality2026 Status
Key NotesSintra
FROZEN in historic centerHeritage protection rationale. Freeze ongoing as of March 2026. Cascais and Oeiras: no major restrictions.
Vila Nova de GaiaSUSPENDED in several districts
2024 moratorium ongoing. Part of Porto metro area. Hotel development prioritised.Évora
OPEN — no current restrictionUNESCO historic walls area under monitoring. Restrictions possible if density grows.
CoimbraOPEN
Student housing regulation is the focus, not tourist AL.Algarve region
MOSTLY OPEN — varies by councilFaro, Albufeira, Lagos, Vilamoura: each has own approach. Verify per-municipality.
Madeira (Funchal)Open outside restricted zones
Regional rules apply. Tourist tax: EUR 2.00/night per person, max 5 nights.Azores
OPENDifferent regional framework. Low tourist tax. Rules vary by island.

________________

sections.title: Privacy Law Compliance — GDPR in Portugal
sections.contentHtml:
Portugal, as an EU member state, is fully subject to the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). The national implementing law, Lei n.º 58/2019, adds several Portugal-specific provisions. Real estate marketplace platforms are classified as data controllers for user registration data and must implement robust consent, processing, and data rights mechanisms.

Governing Framework

* Primary law: GDPR — Regulation (EU) 2016/679, applicable since 25 May 2018.

* National law: Lei n.º 58/2019 of August 8, 2019 — Portuguese Data Protection Law.

* Enforcement body: CNPD (Comissão Nacional de Proteção de Dados).

* Age of consent for data processing: 13 years old in Portugal (Article 24, Law 58/2019).

* Data breach notification: notify CNPD when breach presents risk to fundamental rights of data subjects.

* Documentation requirement: ALL breaches must be documented internally even if formal CNPD notification is not required.

Key Platform Obligations

* Privacy by design: required — CNPD Guidance 2023/1 on organisational and security measures applies.

* Record of processing activities: maintain and make available to CNPD on request.

* Legal basis: a valid legal basis must exist for every processing operation (consent, contract, legitimate interest, etc.).

* Cookie consent: explicit prior consent required under Law 41/2004. Pre-ticked boxes are NOT valid.

* Marketing communications: all unsolicited commercial communications require a valid legal basis. High-risk sector per CNPD.

* Data Governance Act: Decree-Law 2/2025 (January 23, 2025) implements EU Data Governance Act (Regulation 2022/868) in Portugal.

GDPR Fine Structure

Violation TypeMaximum Fine
ExamplesSerious (Art. 83(5) GDPR)
EUR 20M or 4% global annual turnoverProcessing without legal basis, data subject rights violations, international transfer violations
Less serious (Art. 83(4) GDPR)EUR 10M or 2% global annual turnover
Technical/organisational measure failures, DPO obligation failuresNational — large company (Lei 58/2019)
EUR 5,000 to EUR 20,000 or 4% global turnoverNational derogation offences

Data Subject Rights

All of the following rights must be honoured on request. Platforms must build mechanisms to action each:

* Right to be informed (Articles 13–14 GDPR)

* Right of access — including provision of a copy of personal data (Article 15)

* Right to rectification of inaccurate data (Article 16)

* Right to erasure / right to be forgotten (Article 17)

* Right to restriction of processing (Article 18)

* Right to data portability (Article 20)

* Right to object to processing (Article 21)

sections.title: Key Property Documents Reference
sections.contentHtml:
Portuguese NameEnglish Name
Issued ByPurpose
Certidão PermanentePermanent Land Registry Certificate
Conservatória do Registo Predial (Land Registry)Proves ownership and any encumbrances, mortgages, or debts. Available online via predial.pt.
Caderneta PredialProperty Tax Booklet
AT — Portal das FinançasShows official VPT, property classification, and area data.
Licença de UtilizaçãoUser / Habitation Licence
Municipality (Câmara Municipal)Confirms building is approved for intended use. Required for buildings built after August 1951.
Ficha Técnica de HabitaçãoBuilding Data Sheet
Developer/architect — filed with municipalityTechnical specifications. Required if user licence was issued after 30 March 2004.
Certificado EnergéticoEnergy Performance Certificate (EPC)
Certified energy auditor (perito qualificado)MANDATORY for all property sales and rental listings. Classes A+ (best) to F (worst). Valid for 10 years.
Certidão de InfraestruturasInfrastructure Certificate
MunicipalityRequired if a Plot Partitioning Licence was registered since 1992.
ProcuraçãoPower of Attorney
NotaryAuthorises a representative (typically a lawyer) to sign on the buyer's behalf. Common for remote purchases.
NIFFiscal Identification Number
AT — Finanças officeRequired before any contract, bank account opening, or property ownership. Mandatory for all buyers, sellers, and AL operators.

sections.title: Visa and Residency — Property Context
sections.contentHtml:
IMPORTANT: Property purchase alone does NOT grant residency or citizenship in Portugal. The following visas are available to foreign buyers but are separate from the purchase itself.

Visa / ProgrammeDescription
Property Requirement2026 Status
Golden Visa (ARI)Residency by investment. Now limited to non-residential/commercial real estate.
Residential property in Lisbon, Porto, or most coastal areas NO LONGER qualifies for ARI (since 2022 reform).Active but restricted. Verify current eligible investment categories with AIMA.
D7 Passive Income VisaFor retirees and passive income earners. No investment threshold.
None — but property ownership is common among applicants.Active
D8 Digital Nomad VisaFor remote workers employed by non-Portuguese companies or clients.
None required.Active
NHR / IFICITax incentive for new residents. NHR replaced by IFICI in 2024.
None — tax residence based on habitual residence.IFICI active from 2024. NHR closed for new applicants.

sections.title: Universal Compliance Checklist
sections.contentHtml:
A mature compliance programme for a Portugal marketplace platform must integrate the following controls across technical, legal, and operational functions.

AreaVerification Step
FrequencyAgent AMI License
Verify AMI number active on IMPIC. Confirm insurance valid.On agency onboarding
AMI DisplayAMI number shown on all agency listings on all portals.
Per listingEnergy Certificate
Valid EPC present. Class displayed on listing. Check expiry.Per listing
NIF VerificationBuyer and seller NIF obtained before contract signing.
Per transactionCertidão Permanente
Land registry certificate pulled. No blocking encumbrances.Before CPCV
IMT PaymentIMT paid via Portal das Finanças before deed signing.
Before EscrituraStamp Duty
0.8% paid to notary at deed signing.At Escritura
Land Registry FilingOwnership registered post-deed at Conservatória.
After EscrituraAL License
AL registration number obtained and displayed on STR listing.Before first guest
AL InsuranceValid commercial landlord insurance. Proof within 3 days if requested.
Annual checkAL Guest Registration
All non-Portuguese guests registered via SIBA (AIMA) within 3 days.Per stay
Tourist TaxCorrect rate collected and remitted monthly to correct municipality.
MonthlyAL Containment Check
Verify property not in municipal containment zone before AL application.Before AL application
GDPR ConsentMarketing opt-in obtained before listing alerts or newsletters sent.
Before first sendCookie Consent
Explicit consent banner active and compliant on platform.Live on site
DPO RegistrationDPO contact registered with CNPD if company is required to designate one.
At designation
